p.seg: To all sons and faithful people of Holy Mother Church, present and future, Ness of London sends Greeting:

Know that I have granted, and by my present charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh and to the canons serving God there now and in the future, for the salvation of my soul and of my wife’s, and for the salvation of the souls of my lords, and my predecessors and successors, the mill of Linton, which Ness my predecessor gave to the same canons, and made firm by his charter, with the easement of the Tyne Water and the messuage assigned to them. For them to hold in pure and perpetual alms, in every ecclesiastical liberty, just as they hold and posses alms from any gift, freely, quietly, fully and honourably, reserving the multure of my mills, provided that none of my own men might mill at the mill of the foresaid canons without the permission of the canons’ miller. If the miller of the foresaid canons mills any grain at the foresaid mill except the canons’ grain and the grain of their own men, I and my heirs shall receive a half of the whole of that multure.
With these witnesses, Walter of Lindsay, William Noble, Walter of Congleton, Alan de Crahoc, Robert son of Patrick, Jocelin of Fortune, William of Carmichael, and many others.
